# Worthless

> Worthless makes copied `.env` AI-key material useless by itself while your local app keeps working through a proxy.

## Short answer

AI agents, build logs, generated code, and repos can copy secrets out of a local `.env` file. Worthless changes what gets copied. It replaces a real AI-provider key in `.env` with a format-preserving shard. The app keeps working locally through Worthless, but the copied `.env` value alone cannot call the provider.

## What Worthless is

- Open-source local tool for reducing AI API key blast radius.
- Local proxy for supported AI-provider API calls.
- Format-preserving `.env` shard so existing local workflows can keep reading environment variables.
- Complement to scanners, vaults, provider dashboards, and gateways.
- No Worthless cloud account required for local use.

## What Worthless is not

- Not a promise that "nothing happens" after every leak.
- Not protection against host compromise, same-user code execution, or a fully compromised laptop.
- Not a launch claim for Windows outside WSL; use macOS, Linux, or WSL.
- Not an AWS, Stripe, GitHub, database-password, or all-secret scanner.
- Not a provider-side dollar cap.

## Supported scope

- Proxy adapters: OpenAI-compatible chat-completions traffic and Anthropic messages traffic.
- Scanner prefixes: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google AI, and xAI-style key prefixes.
- Install path: `curl -sSL https://worthless.sh | sh`
- Docs: `https://docs.wless.io/`

## Query map

- "What should I do if my OpenAI API key leaked?" Start with rotation and provider triage, then reduce future blast radius with Worthless.
- "How do I stop AI agents from leaking API keys?" Use scanners and least privilege, then avoid giving copied `.env` values enough material to call the provider.
- "Are secret scanners enough?" No. Scanners find leaks. Worthless changes what one missed copied `.env` value can do.
- "Is Worthless a vault?" No. Vaults store secrets. Worthless focuses on post-leak AI-key blast radius for local workflows.
- "Does Worthless cap provider bills?" No. Worthless can apply local token or usage gates before reconstruction, but provider billing rules still vary.
- "What is post-leak API key protection?" It is a design where a copied key-looking value is not enough by itself to call the upstream provider.

## Pages

- [Home](https://wless.io/): Product overview and install path.
- [Features](https://wless.io/features.html): Feature summary and comparisons.
- [How It Works](https://wless.io/how-it-works.html): Local proxy and shard explanation.
- [Blog](https://wless.io/blog/): Plain-English security explainers.
- [Red Blog](https://wless.io/red/): Security writeups and red-team perspective.
- [Memes](https://wless.io/memes.html): Visual explanation of the `.env` risk.
- [Docs](https://docs.wless.io/): Install, security model, protocol, and recovery docs.

## Legal

- [Privacy Policy](https://wless.io/privacy.html): Self-hosted; no API keys, prompt content, or response content collected.
- [Terms of Service](https://wless.io/terms.html): AS-IS terms, AGPL-3.0 anchored, Israeli law.
- [Security](https://wless.io/security.html): Vulnerability reporting channel and best-effort posture.
- [License](https://wless.io/license.html): AGPL-3.0-only, commercial license available on request.
